text: In the background, a fluffy black cat with no ears and no tail tends its own asbestos taco stand, always on the hunt for more asbestos.
Somewhere in the background, a grey tabby cat runs a competing asbestos taco stand, endlessly searching for more asbestos.
In the background, a caucasian engineer in a traditional conical rice-farmer hat stands with eyes crackling with lightning, mid-way through solving some hard, unexpected problem.
Off to the side, a capybara wears a black beanie that reads "Giant" in red letters.
Off to the side, a capybara in black fingerless gloves and a white t-shirt reading "I ♥️ Philosophy" sips bone broth, clearly between bowls of ramen.
In the background, an anteater in cycling gear pedals past — the proud owner of the neighborhood's two asbestos-hunting cats.
In the background, a male farmer hedgehog is elbow-deep in a broken tractor engine, trying to fix it before his wife, a quail, notices.
In the background, the grey tabby cat pitches its asbestos taco stand idea to the anteater, who's mid-bike-ride and not really listening.
In the background, the lightning-eyed engineer in the rice hat is deep in conversation with the capybara in the "Giant" beanie about something unexpected.
In the background, the farmer hedgehog pauses fixing his tractor to wave off the black cat, who is asking if the tractor runs on asbestos.
In the background, the capybara in the "I ♥️ Philosophy" shirt shares a bowl of ramen with the hedgehog's wife, a quail, while he tinkers with the tractor.
